hyperedges.label: DKB FinTS connection test pipeline: config loading, redaction, and live client execution
Prisma demo seeding flow: flag guard, user/household/category/transaction creation
Settings page auth flow: register, login, logout server actions with rate limiting and session management
All feature pages share the FeaturePage/AppShell UI scaffold pattern
DKB configuration, connection status, and connector descriptor form the DKB integration status pipeline
Session management and rate limiting together form the authentication security boundary
CSV Import Pipeline: parse → dedup hash → persist via prisma → provenance batch
Docker deployment stack: compose → app service → postgres service → prisma db init
Auth guard pattern: getCurrentViewer → getViewerHouseholdContext → redirect if unauthenticated

links.source_location: prisma scripts delegate to Prisma CLI which reads prisma.config.ts
registerAction uses hashPassword from lib/auth/password; seed.ts defines its own hashPassword — same purpose, different implementations
Both solve env-var loading for external service credentials (DKB FinTS vs Prisma DATABASE_URL) without a shared code path
Next.js with turbopack uses tsconfig paths for module resolution
eslint-config-next targets .next build output and Next.js conventions
children prop wraps all pages including page.tsx
page.tsx references dkb connector, settings page references auth — both part of the same Next.js app routing
